attachment.snippet:
1	//! User-level config loaded from `~/.config/vc-x1/config.toml`.
2	//!
3	//! Backs init's account/repo-target resolution. No magic
4	//! fallbacks — a missing file or missing key produces a
5	//! predictable error pointing at the exact key to set.
6	//!
7	//! Schema (multi-account, dotted keys for compactness):
8	//!
9	//! ```toml
10	//! [default]
11	//! account = "home"      # default --account when absent
12	//! debug   = "trace"     # default --debug value when used without arg
13	//!
14	//! [account.home]
15	//! repo.default          = "remote"                       # default --repo cat when absent
16	//! repo.category.remote  = "git@github.com:winksaville"   # value for --repo remote (no =val)
17	//! repo.category.local   = "~/test-fixtures"              # value for --repo local (no =val)
18	//!
19	//! [account.work]
20	//! repo.default          = "remote"
21	//! repo.category.remote  = "git@github.com:anthropic"
22	//! repo.category.local   = "/work/fixtures"
23	//! ```
24	//!
25	//! Three-layer resolution — see
26	//! `notes/chores/chores-08.md > User config (0.41.1-3, redesigned in 0.41.1-4)`:
27	//!
28	//! 1. account: CLI `--account` → `[default].account` → error.
29	//! 2. category: CLI `--repo <cat>` → `[account.<a>.repo].default`
30	//!    → error.
31	//! 3. value: CLI `--repo <cat>=<val>` →
32	//!    `[account.<a>.repo.category].<cat>` → error.
33	//!
34	//! Values are **literal targets**, not section-name pointers.
35	//! For built-in `category = "remote"` the value is a URL prefix
36	//! (init appends `/<NAME>.git`); for `category = "local"` it's
37	//! the parent dir for fixture bare repos.
38	//!
39	//! `-3` shipped a flat first-cut (`[default] repo-remote-provider`,
40	//! `[github] owner`); `-4` is the redesign before any consumer
41	//! is wired in. `-5` (init reshape) is the first consumer.
42	
43	use std::collections::HashMap;
44	use std::path::{Path, PathBuf};
45	
46	use log::debug;
47	
48	use crate::toml_simple;
49	
50	/// Per-account configuration.
51	///
52	/// - `repo_default` — `[account.<name>.repo].default` — the
53	///   category to use when `--repo` is absent.
54	/// - `repo_category` — `[account.<name>.repo.category]` — map
55	///   from category name (e.g. `"remote"`, `"local"`) to its
56	///   literal value (URL prefix, fixture parent dir, etc.).
57	#[derive(Debug, Default, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]
58	pub struct AccountConfig {
59	    pub repo_default: Option<String>,
60	    pub repo_category: HashMap<String, String>,
61	}
62	
63	/// Resolved user config.
64	///
65	/// All fields are optional or default-empty — absent file or
66	/// absent keys yield empty values. Consumers apply their own
67	/// errors at use time.
68	///
69	/// **Two write modes** for repo defaults:
70	/// - **Top-level `[repo]`** — single-account shorthand. Sits in
71	///   `top_level_repo`. Used when neither `--account` nor
72	///   `[default].account` resolves to a known account.
73	/// - **`[account.<name>]`** — multi-account. Sits in `accounts`.
74	///   Selected by `--account` CLI or `[default].account`.
75	///
76	/// Mixing both is rejected at load time (ambiguous which one
77	/// init should consult).
78	#[derive(Debug, Default, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]
79	pub struct UserConfig {
80	    /// `[default].account` — default account when `--account`
81	    /// is absent.
82	    pub default_account: Option<String>,
83	
84	    /// `[default].debug` — default value when `--debug` is used
85	    /// without an argument. (Reserved; not currently consumed.)
86	    pub default_debug: Option<String>,
87	
88	    /// Top-level `[repo]` — single-account shorthand. `Some`
89	    /// only when the file has top-level `repo.*` keys and no
90	    /// `[account.*]` sections.
91	    pub top_level_repo: Option<AccountConfig>,
92	
93	    /// Per-account configuration, keyed by account name.
94	    pub accounts: HashMap<String, AccountConfig>,
95	
96	    /// `[bot-session].items` — comma-separated default item list
97	    /// for `bot-session` (e.g. `"headers,user,assistant,tool,summary"`).
98	    /// Parsed and validated by the bot-session op, not here.
99	    pub bot_session_items: Option<String>,
100	}
101	
102	/// CLI selector for `--repo <cat>[=<val>]`.
103	///
104	/// - `--repo` absent → `None`.
105	/// - `--repo <cat>` → `Some({ category: cat, value: None })`.
106	/// - `--repo <cat>=<val>` → `Some({ category: cat, value: Some(val) })`.
107	#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]
108	pub struct RepoSelector {
109	    pub category: String,
110	    pub value: Option<String>,
111	}
112	
113	/// Path to the user config file.
114	///
115	/// - Honors `$XDG_CONFIG_HOME` if set (XDG Base Directory spec).
116	/// - Falls back to `$HOME/.config`.
117	/// - Errors if neither variable is set.
118	pub fn config_path() -> Result<PathBuf, Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
119	    let base = if let Some(xdg) = std::env::var_os("XDG_CONFIG_HOME") {
120	        PathBuf::from(xdg)
121	    } else if let Some(home) = std::env::var_os("HOME") {
122	        PathBuf::from(home).join(".config")
123	    } else {
124	        return Err("neither $XDG_CONFIG_HOME nor $HOME is set".into());
125	    };
126	    Ok(base.join("vc-x1").join("config.toml"))
127	}
128	
129	/// Load the user config from the default location.
130	///
131	/// - Missing file → empty `UserConfig`.
132	/// - Malformed file → fatal error.
133	pub fn load() -> Result<UserConfig, Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
134	    load_from(&config_path()?)
135	}
136	
137	/// Load the user config from an explicit path.
138	///
139	/// - Missing file → empty `UserConfig`.
140	/// - Malformed file → fatal error (propagated from `toml_simple`).
141	/// - Unknown sections / keys are silently ignored
142	///   (forward-compatible with future schema additions).
143	pub fn load_from(path: &Path) -> Result<UserConfig, Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
144	    if !path.exists() {
145	        debug!(
146	            "config: file not found at {} — using empty UserConfig",
147	            path.display()
148	        );
149	        return Ok(UserConfig::default());
150	    }
151	    debug!("config: loading from {}", path.display());
152	    let map = toml_simple::toml_load(path)?;
153	
154	    let mut cfg = UserConfig {
155	        default_account: map.get("default.account").cloned(),
156	        default_debug: map.get("default.debug").cloned(),
157	        top_level_repo: None,
158	        accounts: HashMap::new(),
159	        bot_session_items: map.get("bot-session.items").cloned(),
160	    };
161	
162	    let mut top_level = AccountConfig::default();
163	    let mut top_level_seen = false;
164	
165	    // Walk all keys; route `repo.*` to top-level, `account.<n>.…`
166	    // to per-account.
167	    for (full_key, value) in &map {
168	        if let Some(suffix) = full_key.strip_prefix("repo.") {
169	            top_level_seen = true;
170	            apply_repo_subkey(&mut top_level, suffix, value);
171	            continue;
172	        }
173	        let Some(rest) = full_key.strip_prefix("account.") else {
174	            continue;
175	        };
176	        let Some((name, suffix)) = rest.split_once('.') else {
177	            continue;
178	        };
179	        let Some(repo_suffix) = suffix.strip_prefix("repo.") else {
180	            continue; // unknown sub-section under [account.<n>]
181	        };

content.text:
`0.70.0-3` work complete. Please review.

What landed:
- **The item model**: output is now eight items — `headers, user, assistant, tool, thinking, results, meta, summary` — each with `--<item>` / `--no-<item>` (last-wins via clap `overrides_with`), plus `--all` / `--none` as bulk bases. Resolution: base = `--all`/`--none` if given, else the config list, else the built-in default (`headers, user, assistant, tool, summary`); per-item toggles then adjust. `ItemSet` has `NONE`/`ALL`/`BUILTIN` consts; `RenderOptions` is gone.
- **Config**: `~/.config/vc-x1/config.toml` gains `[bot-session] items = "user,summary"` (comma-separated string — `toml_simple` has no arrays). Unknown item names error with the valid list. `ctx.user_config` is finally consumed by the op.
- **Renderer gating**: every emission is gated on its item; `--no-headers` keeps blank turn separators (and the turn count) but drops the `===` lines; summary off suppresses the trailing line entirely.
- **Tests**: unit — `item_gating` (headers-off, user-only, tool-off), `resolve_items_cases` (all bases, CLI-over-config, unknown name, empty list), config parse test in config.rs; CLI — `--none --user` minimal view, `--no-tool --no-headers`, and a config-items test using the fixture's isolated HOME (with `XDG_CONFIG_HOME` pinned). Existing tests reworked to `ItemSet`.
- `src/main.rs` long_about rewritten around the items model; `src/config.rs` struct/literals extended.

Validation: fmt/clippy clean, all 6 suites green (12 bot_session unit + 7 CLI tests), installed `0.70.0-3`; end-to-end runs shown above (`--none --user`, `--no-headers`).

One design note: with `--none` and no items, output is empty and exit is 0 — no warning. And `--headers`-only shows headers of turns that have *renderable content* in the set… which is none, so it prints nothing; headers only appear alongside at least one content item. That's inherent to the lazy-header design; flag it if you'd rather headers render standalone.

content.text: Nice — hide the `--no-*` entries and let the heading teach the pattern:

input.command:
python3 - <<'EOF'
import re
p = 'src/bot_session.rs'
s = open(p).read()
# heading rename on all "Items listed" args
s = s.replace('help_heading = "Items listed"', 'help_heading = "Items listed (--no-<item> to disable)"')
# hide the --no-* flags: their arg attribute is the one whose overrides_with names the positive
for item in ["headers", "user", "assistant", "tool", "thinking", "results", "meta", "summary"]:
    s = s.replace(
        f'#[arg(long, help_heading = "Items listed (--no-<item> to disable)", overrides_with = "{item}")]',
        f'#[arg(long, hide = true, help_heading = "Items listed (--no-<item> to disable)", overrides_with = "{item}")]',
    )
open(p, 'w').write(s)
EOF

        help_heading = "Items listed (--no-<item> to disable)",
        overrides_with = "no_summary"
    )]
    pub summary: bool,
    /// Do not list the trailing summary line
    #[arg(
        long,
        hide = true,
        help_heading = "Items listed (--no-<item> to disable)",
        overrides_with = "summary"
    )]
    pub no_summary: bool,

"""
assert summary_block in s, "summary block not found"
s = s.replace(summary_block, "")
headers_start = """    /// List turn headers (=== role time ===) [default: on]"""
assert headers_start in s
s = s.replace(headers_start, summary_block + headers_start)
open(p, 'w').write(s)
EOF

message.content: Ok, lets touch on config you suggested an different one for these I thought there wasn't a need, also are there tests that CLI overrides config and that config works?

content.text:
On the "do we need two configs" question — here's the state and the case:

**What's implemented**: two config layers plus CLI, most specific wins — CLI toggles > workspace `.vc-config.toml` `[bot-session].items` > user `~/.config/vc-x1/config.toml` `[bot-session].items` > built-in default.

**Why I kept the user-config layer**: it's the "my taste, every workspace" knob — set `items = "…"` once and every project honors it without editing each repo's committed `.vc-config.toml` (which collaborators would inherit). The workspace layer is the "this project's convention" knob. That said, if you consider machine-wide defaults not worth the concept count, the user-config layer is a small, isolated piece (one `UserConfig` field + one test + one `.or()` in resolution) — easy to drop now, and just as easy to add back later. Your call; I'd keep it.

**Test coverage — yes, both directions are covered**:
- `cli_bot_session_config_items` — user config *works* (config `items = "user,summary"` visibly changes the default view) and *CLI overrides it* (`--tool` adds over the config set).
- `cli_bot_session_workspace_items` — workspace `.vc-config.toml` works and *beats the user config* (both present, workspace wins), and *CLI beats the workspace* (`--assistant` restores what the workspace list omitted).
- Unit level, `resolve_items_cases` — config list parsing, CLI-toggle-over-config-base, unknown item name errors, empty list allowed.

So the full precedence chain has a test at each link. Keep both layers as-is, or drop the user-config one before we commit?

message.content:
Keep both layers I misunderstood previously, yes we want ~/.config/vc-x1/config.toml and the hierarchy we have!
You picked out what items to have, but what is the "full" list as of today and how did you decide on this list?

content.text:
The full list today (8 items):

| item | what it emits | default |
|---|---|---|
| `summary` | the trailing stats line | on |
| `headers` | `=== role HH:MM:SS ===` turn headers | on |
| `user` | typed user prompts | on |
| `assistant` | assistant reply text | on |
| `tool` | `[tool] Name: gist` call one-liners | on |
| `thinking` | `[thinking]` blocks | off |
| `results` | `[result]` / `[result:error]` lines | off |
| `meta` | meta user lines, `--- system … ---` lines, sidechain entries | off |

How I decided: the rule was **one item per visually distinct kind of line the renderer can emit**, derived from what's actually in the data. The transcript gives us three renderable entry types (user, assistant, system) whose content blocks break down into prompt text, assistant text, tool_use, tool_result, and thinking — each of those became an item because each is independently useful to include or exclude. `headers` and `summary` are renderer-generated rather than data-derived, but they're distinct emissions too, so they got items (that's what makes `--none --user` a clean prompts-only stream possible).

Two deliberate judgment calls:

- **`meta` is a bundle**, not one data kind: `isMeta` user lines + `system` entries + `isSidechain` entries. I bundled them because all three are "not the main conversation" and, today, you rarely want one without the others. The likely future split is `sidechain` getting its own item when the cross-file cycle lands and subagent conversations become first-class — at that point "show me the subagent's work" is a real request distinct from "show me injected context".
- **What's *not* an item**: the ~11 bookkeeping entry types (`file-history-snapshot`, `progress`, `permission-mode`, …) are never rendered at all — they're state snapshots, not conversation, and the deferred `--raw` mode (Todo #12) is the right way to see them. Elision markers and malformed-line warnings are mechanics of the view, not content, so they're not toggleable either.

The plain version: I looked at every different kind of line the command can print, and gave each kind its own on/off switch — eight in all. Things that are really three related kinds got one shared switch (`meta`) until there's a reason to split them, and file-bookkeeping records that were never printed in the first place didn't get a switch at all.

If that taxonomy looks right, we're ready for the `-3` description review and push.

message.content: So actually "Z" time do you know if that's always the case?

content.text:
Honestly: no — I know it's true of *this dataset* (all 56,426 timestamps), and I have a strong mechanism inference, but not documentation. The ECMAScript spec guarantees `Date.toISOString()` always emits UTC with a `Z` suffix, and Claude Code is a Node app where that's the idiomatic call — so if that's how they write it (we think it is), always-Z follows. But Anthropic doesn't document the transcript format, and a future version could switch to offset timestamps (`+02:00`) without notice.

That exposes a real flaw in what I just wrote: I append `Z` unconditionally, so a hypothetical `2026-07-17T04:17:09+02:00` would be sliced and *mislabeled* as Zulu — worse than no label. Fixing it to only claim Z when the source actually says Z, and otherwise show the raw timestamp (offset intact):

input.description: Only label Z when source is Z; pass other forms verbatim

content.text:
Fixed — the display now claims `Z` only when the source timestamp actually ends in `Z`; any other shape (an offset form like `+02:00`, or something short) passes through verbatim, never sliced and relabeled. A unit test pins the pass-through. The doc comment records the distinction: always-Z is *observed* (all 56k lines to date), not a documented guarantee. All suites green, installed.

The plain version: today every timestamp in the files is UTC, so you'll always see the `Z` form; if Anthropic ever changes the format, the viewer will show their new timestamp exactly as written instead of guessing wrong.

content.text:
**Title:**
```
feat: bot-session item flags + config defaults
```

**Body:**
```
Work (0.70.0-3): bot-session output becomes a composable item
set — headers, user, assistant, tool, thinking, results,
meta, summary — giving the user complete control over what a
transcript view contains. Item defaults resolve git-style,
most specific wins: CLI toggles > workspace .vc-config.toml >
user config > built-in. Validated: fmt/clippy clean, full
suite green (13 bot_session unit + 12 CLI tests), installed
0.70.0-3; spot-checked --none --user, --no-headers, and
--no-all/--no-none on real transcripts.

- src/bot_session.rs: reworked around the item model
  - each item toggled by --<item> / --no-<item> (last-wins
    via clap overrides_with; --no-* hidden from help — the
    "Items listed (--no-<item> to disable)" heading teaches
    the pattern); --summary listed first, defaults-on before
    defaults-off
  - --all / --none bulk bases, with hidden aliases --no-all
    (= --none) and --no-none (= --all) completing the
    convention
  - resolution: --all/--none base, else workspace
    [bot-session].items (.vc-config.toml via
    find_workspace_root; malformed file errors, absent is
    fine), else user-config items, else the built-in default
    (headers, user, assistant, tool, summary); per-item
    toggles adjust the resolved set
  - ItemSet (NONE/ALL/BUILTIN consts) replaces
    RenderOptions; every renderer emission gated on its
    item; headers-off keeps blank turn separators and turn
    counts; summary-off drops the trailing line
  - turn headers carry the full UTC date-time (=== user
    2026-07-17 04:17:09Z ===) — needed once views span days
    or concatenate sessions; the Z is claimed only when the
    source timestamp ends in Z (observed always, all 56k
    lines to date, but undocumented) — any other shape
    passes through verbatim rather than being mislabeled
- src/config.rs: [bot-session].items key (comma-separated
  string — toml_simple has no arrays) on UserConfig; parse
  test; ctx.user_config now consumed by bot-session
- src/bot_session/tests.rs: item_gating,
  resolve_items_cases, and non-Z timestamp pass-through
  added; existing tests reworked to ItemSet
- tests/cli_bot_session.rs: --none --user minimal view,
  --no-tool --no-headers subtraction, all-eight --no-<item>
  sweep and its --none + all-eight positive mirror, pair
  last-wins both orders, --no-all/--no-none aliases,
  user-config items test, workspace-items precedence test
  (workspace beats user config, CLI beats workspace;
  isolated HOME + XDG_CONFIG_HOME pinned)
- src/init/tests.rs: UserConfig literals gain the new field
- src/main.rs: bot-session long_about rewritten around the
  items model
- Cargo.toml, Cargo.lock: version 0.70.0-3
- TODO.md: -3 rung added to the ladder and flipped to done
```

May I commit and push?

content.content:
## Usage

```
vc-x1 list [-r REVISION] [-n COMMITS]  # List commits in a jj repo
vc-x1 desc [-r REVISION] [-n COMMITS]  # Show full description of a commit
vc-x1 chid [-r REVISION] [-n COMMITS]  # Print changeID(s) for a revision
vc-x1 show [-r REVISION] [-n COMMITS]  # Show commit details and diff summary
vc-x1 validate-desc [OPTS]                 # Validate commit descriptions
vc-x1 fix-desc [OPTS]                     # Fix commit descriptions (dry-run default)
vc-x1 validate-todo [FILE]                # Check todo-file entry numbering
vc-x1 fix-todo [FILE]                     # Renumber todo file (dry-run default)
vc-x1 validate-bot [OPTS]                 # Check the bot repo is published
vc-x1 clone <REPO> [NAME] [OPTS]          # Clone a dual-repo project
vc-x1 init <TARGET> [OPTS]                # Create a new dual-repo project
vc-x1 symlink [TARGET] [OPTS]             # Create Claude Code project symlink
vc-x1 sync [OPTS]                          # Fetch + sync both repos to their remotes
vc-x1 revert [OPTS]                        # Restore repos to a prior operation
vc-x1 squash-push [BOOKMARK] [OPTS]        # Squash @ into @-, advance a bookmark, push
vc-x1 push [BOOKMARK] [OPTS]               # Commit both repos, push work, squash-push bot
vc-x1 --version                            # Print version
vc-x1 --help                           # Print help
```

**The `..` notation:** dots on the revision show which direction to
list commits:

- `x..` — x at top, ancestors below (older commits)
- `..x` — descendants above (newer commits), x at bottom
- `..x..` — both directions, x in the middle

COMMITS is the total number of commits to show (x is always included).
Without dots, `x` with a count defaults to `x..` (ancestors).
For `..x..`, the budget is split: ancestors get the extra on odd counts.

```
vc-x1 list -r @              # just @ (1 commit)
vc-x1 list -r @.. -n 5       # @ + 4 ancestors (5 commits, @ at top)
vc-x1 list -r ..@ -n 3       # 2 descendants + @ (3 commits, @ at bottom)
vc-x1 list -r ..@.. -n 3     # 1 descendant, @, 1 ancestor (3 commits)
vc-x1 list -r ..@.. -n 4     # 1 descendant, @, 2 ancestors (4 commits)
```

### Shell completion

vc-x1 provides tab completion for all subcommands and flags using claps
[unstable-dynamic](https://docs.rs/clap_complete/latest/clap_complete/env/struct.CompleteEnv.html)
only feature. It is a "simple" implementation and does not handle completing
revisions but still useful. To enable it, add one of the following to your shell's startup file:

```bash
===
vc-x1 fix-todo --no-dry-run

# Operate on a specific file
vc-x1 fix-todo path/to/todo.md
```

| Flag | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `--no-dry-run` | Write the renumbered file in place [default: dry-run] |

### validate-bot

Check the bot repo is in its expected at-rest state: `main`
matching `main@origin`, with its remote refs tracked. At rest the
two always match — the bookmark only moves inside a `push` /
`squash-push` run, which publishes it in the same invocation — so
a mismatch means an earlier publish was lost. Read-only and cheap
(two `jj` lookups, no build steps); exits non-zero on any finding
and fixes nothing — resolve with `vc-x1 squash-push -R <bot-repo>`.

```
# Check ./.claude (run from the project root)
vc-x1 validate-bot

# Explicit bot-repo path
vc-x1 validate-bot -R path/to/.claude

| Flag | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `--no-dry-run` | Write the renumbered file in place [default: dry-run] |

### validate-bot

Check the bot repo is in its expected at-rest state: `main`
matching `main@origin`, with its remote refs tracked. At rest the
two always match — the bookmark only moves inside a `push` /
`squash-push` run, which publishes it in the same invocation — so
a mismatch means an earlier publish was lost. Read-only and cheap
(two `jj` lookups, no build steps); exits non-zero on any finding
and fixes nothing — resolve with `vc-x1 squash-push -R <bot-repo>`.

```
# Check ./.claude (run from the project root)
vc-x1 validate-bot

# Explicit bot-repo path
vc-x1 validate-bot -R path/to/.claude

file.content:
# Notes

This directory contains various notes and documentation related to the project.
Each file is organized by topic for easy reference.

By default there are the chores-NN.md files in
[chores/](chores). Chores are general notes about tasks;
short term tasks and their status live at the repo root in
[../TODO.md](../TODO.md). The chores-NN files are numbered
in sequence; the highest-numbered file is the active one,
older ones are closed.

Tool architecture — the CLI-args / `Context`+`Params` split, a
generic module map, the subcommand model, and what the
Context+Params port and the options_flags extraction *are* (the
*live status* is in the chores files) — lives at the repo root in
[`../ARCHITECTURE.md`](../ARCHITECTURE.md).

Multi-cycle programs too big for a TODO.md entry get their own
dated plan file — e.g.
[refactor-20260716.md](refactor-20260716.md) (typed jj facade →
jj-lib in-process): one `##` section per stage, so chores and
todo entries can reference a stage by anchor. The plan file owns
the forward design; chores still records each shipped cycle.

In the future we I expect we may want to create a "notes"
database to better manage the information, TBD.

Examples chore file:
```
# Chores-01.md
 
General maintenance tasks and considerations for the project see other files for
more specific topics. A chore in a chores file provides quick information on the
how and why of a particular chore.

## Create a binary that lists jj info 

This binary should list the changeID, commitID, and description title
and using `jj-lib`
```

## Workflow and conventions

Bot-facing workflow and conventions live in
[`../AGENTS.md`](../AGENTS.md):

- [Notes file conventions](../AGENTS.md#notes-file-conventions)
  — Todo format, Reference numbering, Notes references
  (`[[N]]` citation style), Markdown anchor links, Retiring
  Done entries.
- [Chores conventions](../AGENTS.md#chores-conventions) —
  section headers / Done entries exact-title rule, content
  rules, `Commits:` line format.
- [Code Conventions](../AGENTS.md#code-conventions) — doc
  comments, `// OK: …` on `unwrap*` calls, ask-on-ambiguity,
  stuck detection.

Per-cycle workflow lives in
[`cycle-protocol.md`](cycle-protocol.md):

- [Cycles](cycle-protocol.md#cycles) — three-phase shape
  (Preparation → Work → Close-out), `X.Y.Z-N` numbering,
  sub-cycles.
- [Per-commit flow](cycle-protocol.md#per-commit-flow) —
  cargo cycle (`fmt` / `clippy` / `test` / `install`),
  work + commit description review gates.
- [Commit description](cycle-protocol.md#commit-description)
  — Conventional Commits + `(version)` suffix; body shape
  per work vs bot repo.
- [Pushing](cycle-protocol.md#pushing) — push policy,
  close-out shape, `.claude` cadence.

input.old_string: ### validate-desc
input.new_string:
### bot-session

Display a Claude Code session transcript
(`.claude/<uuid>.jsonl`) as a readable conversation. The
transcript format is undocumented and evolves; parsing is
tolerant — unknown entry types are skipped and counted,
malformed lines (e.g. a live session's truncated last line)
warn to stderr and never fail the run.

Output is composed of eight **items**, each independently
toggleable:

| Item | Emits | Default |
|------|-------|---------|
| `summary` | trailing stats line (shown / hidden / skipped counts) | on |
| `headers` | `=== role 2026-07-17 04:17:09Z ===` turn headers (UTC) | on |
| `user` | typed user prompts | on |
| `assistant` | assistant reply text | on |
| `tool` | `[tool] Name: gist` call one-liners | on |
| `thinking` | `[thinking]` blocks | off |
| `results` | `[result]` / `[result:error]` lines (capped at 10) | off |
| `meta` | meta user lines, `--- system … ---` lines, sidechain entries | off |

Toggle with `--<item>` / `--no-<item>` (last one wins).
`--all` / `--none` reset the base — every item on / off — then
per-item flags adjust (`--no-all` and `--no-none` are aliases
of `--none` and `--all`). Bookkeeping line types
(file-history snapshots, progress markers, …) are never
rendered — they're counted in the summary as "skipped".

Default items resolve git-style, most specific wins:

1. CLI `--<item>` / `--no-<item>` flags
2. workspace `.vc-config.toml` — `[bot-session].items`
3. user `~/.config/vc-x1/config.toml` — `[bot-session].items`
4. built-in: `headers,user,assistant,tool,summary`

```toml
# In ~/.config/vc-x1/config.toml (all your workspaces) or
# <workspace>/.vc-config.toml (this workspace, committed):
[bot-session]
items = "headers,user,assistant,tool,summary"
```

```
# Default conversation view
vc-x1 bot-session .claude/<uuid>.jsonl

# Everything — thinking, tool results, meta/system too
vc-x1 bot-session --all FILE

# Prompts only: what was asked, nothing else
vc-x1 bot-session --none --user FILE

# Default view minus tool calls and headers
vc-x1 bot-session --no-tool --no-headers FILE

# Slices of the rendered output
vc-x1 bot-session --lines 40 FILE      # first 40 lines
vc-x1 bot-session --lines -15 FILE     # last 15 lines
vc-x1 bot-session --lines 100,20 FILE  # 20 lines from Index 100
vc-x1 bot-session --lines 100,-20 FILE # 20 lines ending at Index 100
vc-x1 bot-session --lines 0 FILE       # stats summary only
```

| Flag | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `--<item>` / `--no-<item>` | Add / remove one of the eight items (last one wins) |
| `--all` / `--none` | Base: every item on / off (aliases `--no-none` / `--no-all`) |
| `--lines SPEC` | Slice the rendered lines (0-based Index): `N` first, `-N` last, `I,C` from I, `I,-C` ending at I; `0` = summary only |

Cut points show an `… (N lines skipped)` marker, and a sliced
run's summary leads with `K of M lines shown` so it never
claims more than was displayed. Timestamps are shown as UTC
(`Z`) exactly when the source timestamp carries it — observed
always, but the format is undocumented, so anything else would
pass through verbatim.

### validate-desc

## In Progress

When a `## Todo` item is picked up, its text moves here: the
problem overview and its list of things to do. That is followed
by the "plan" — a bulleted list of the development "ladder":
   - 0.xx.y-0 blah (done)
   - 0.xx.y-1 blah blah (current)
   - 0.xx.y-2 blah blah blah
   - 0.xx.y close-out and validation

**feat: bot-session transcript viewer**

Display one Claude Code session transcript
(`.claude/<uuid>.jsonl`) as a readable conversation — first step
toward seeing all bot sessions and linking prompts to the
changes they produced. Two-layer parse: serde_json as text →
`Value` only (no derive); hand-written extraction into our own
structs that evolve as we learn the format. v1 is file-path in,
conversation view out; index view, session discovery, and
cross-file references (sidechain `agent-*.jsonl`, compaction
chains) come in later cycles. Full design in the approved plan
(bot session of 2026-07-17). v1 uses no version-control code;
when later cycles link prompts to commits (chids, ochid
trailers), they go through jj-lib in-process per the typed
jj facade Todo #1 — no new `run("jj", …)` sites.

- 0.70.0-0 chore: open show-session cycle (done)
- 0.70.0-1 feat: transcript parse + typed layer for show-session (done)
- 0.70.0-2 feat: bot-session command + conversation renderer (done)
  (command renamed from show-session mid-step; the pushed
  -0/-1 titles keep the old stem)
- 0.70.0-3 feat: bot-session item flags + config defaults (done)
  - items: headers, user, assistant, tool, thinking,
    results, meta, summary — each --xxx / --no-xxx
    (last-wins), plus --all / --none bulk toggles
  - built-in default set: headers, user, assistant, tool,
    summary; user config can override via a [bot-session]
    items list; CLI flags adjust the resolved set
- 0.70.0 close-out and validation (current)

## Todo

 Entries are in **strict priority rank** — #1 highest,
 descending. Reprioritize by moving an entry, then
 `vc-x1 fix-todo --no-dry-run TODO.md` to renumber.
 The numbers are positional rank, not stable IDs — to refer
 to a Todo, name it by its **title** (a greppable mention;
 a numbered list item has no anchor to link to), not its
 number. Long-tail entries
 live in [todo-backlog.md](notes/todo-backlog.md). Use the

389:## Done
## Done

Completed tasks are moved from `## Todo` to here, `## Done`, as they are completed
and older `## Done` sections are moved to [done.md](notes/done.md) to keep this file small.

_Migrated to [done.md](notes/done.md) on 2026-07-14 (0.51.0–0.65.2 batch)._

- feat: pin bot repo to main (0.68.0) — `--bookmark` is code-repo-only in push and sync; the session repo's side of every step (tracking preflight, classify/act, `bookmark-set` — renamed from `bookmark-both` — `finalize --push`, completion sanity) is pinned to `main`; plus two mid-cycle sync fixes: `reposition_session` no-ops when `@-` is the `main` tip, and the clean case prints one `nothing to sync` summary line [[23]]
- docs: diagnose silent session-push loss (0.68.1) — Bugs #1 root-caused: push's detached finalize child is killed at sandbox teardown before its delayed squash/push runs, so bot-run pushes never push `.claude`; diagnosis recorded in bugs.md, fix design queued as Todo #1 (inline session push + preflight backstop + finalize as the user's empty-@ tidy-up); 0.68.0 chores `Commits:` backfilled [[25]]
- feat: inline session push + squash-push (0.69.0) — push's session publish is in-process (`squash-push-bot` stage; a failure is a visible push failure — the silent session-push loss fixed); `finalize` renamed to the zero-ceremony `squash-push` (detach / delay / failure markers retired, no alias); new `vc-x1 validate-bot` + an erroring push-preflight backstop enforce the at-rest `main == main@origin` invariant (no auto-fix); push preflight drops the hardcoded cargo steps (vc-x1 assumes nothing about repo contents beyond `.jj` + `.vc-config.toml`); work/bot terminology + stage renames across code and docs, README rewritten (Terminology section, live-validated walkthroughs); crate renamed back to `vc-x1` [[20]]
- docs: shared protocol sync + jj refactor plan — adopted the vc-template-x1 shared notes set (AGENTS.md, cycle-protocol.md, versioning.md, jj-tips.md) with vc-x1's 0.69.0 corrections ratified template-side (manifest: [notes-sync-20260716.md](notes/notes-sync-20260716.md)); jj facade → jj-lib refactor program planned in [refactor-20260716.md](notes/refactor-20260716.md), absorbing eight Todos [[1]]
- docs: move todo.md to root TODO.md — todo list moved from notes/ to the conventional root-file family; live references swept (AGENTS.md, cycle-protocol.md, README, ARCHITECTURE, notes/*); no-arg validate-todo / fix-todo default follows the move; historical files keep `notes/todo.md`; the shared doc set diverges until vc-template-x1 and iiac-perf apply the same change [[2]]

content.content:
140	- apply max review #1 — applied six concerns, four nits, and the process observation from the `max-review-1` working list to the por/dual parity design + copying stub; reframed Todo #1 (push validate body intro), seeded pre-commit-single-rule + `validate-numbering` Todos; working list fully drained, then retired (deleted — git history holds it) (0.62.0) [[107]]
141	- docs: adopt AGENTS.md — rename `CLAUDE.md` → `AGENTS.md` (Zed and the agent-tooling ecosystem default to it); one-line `@AGENTS.md` import shim at `CLAUDE.md` keeps Claude Code auto-loading; live `CLAUDE.md` references repointed to `AGENTS.md` so links resolve in editors and on GitHub; history prose (`chores-01..12` / `done.md`) left as written, with only the 3 navigational anchor links in the `chores-10/11/12` headers repointed (0.63.0) [[108]]
142	- docs: tighten after-finalize rule — rename to "After push or finalize: stop and wait" (both triggers named) and spell out that `vc-x1 push` bundles the push + `vc-x1 finalize` on `.claude` as tail stages, so all closing words go *before* invoking the wrapper and nothing is emitted after it returns (0.63.1) [[109]]
143	- docs: codify merge-non-ff recipe — promote the merge-non-ff close-out recipe to a `### Merge non-ff recipe` subsection in cycle-protocol.md (rebase → `jj new` lift → push + post-hoc caveat); reword `### Shape at close-out push` (work-done framing, Merge non-ff tagged default); standardize jj rebase `-d` → `--onto`/`-o` in AGENTS.md and drop the post-amend `jj new` note (the recipe now owns the empty-`@` why); also clarified the Preparation step (Cargo.lock, In-Progress move wording) (0.64.0) [[110]]
144	- docs: record finalize ochid-loss bug (0.65.1) — bugs.md gains the fc finalize ochid-drop incident as Bugs #1 with the fix queued as Todo #1; fc AGENTS.md additions ported (jj-not-git, one-command-per-invocation, push-injects-trailers, ochid resolvability + `.vc-config.toml`); stale chores-10 "active file" prose genericized in notes/README.md + ARCHITECTURE.md [[111]]
145	- fix: refuse ochid-dropping squash (0.65.2) — `finalize` refuses a squash that would drop source-only `ochid:` trailers (`extract_ochids` / `ochids_at_risk` / `check_squash_keeps_ochids` + tests), guarding in preflight and again in `finalize_exec` after `--delay`; failure-marker surfacing moved after the command's output with a historical banner and the `error=` value flattened; README manual-test section + `support/gen-exmpl-1-3.sh` regenerator [[112]]
146	- feat: reposition @ onto synced bookmark (0.66.0) — after a successful `--no-check` sync, `@` is repositioned onto the just-synced bookmark: code repo `jj new <b>` when clean (or `--rebase`/prompt-gated rebase when dirty; left in place when diverged/ahead), `.claude` always `jj new main` (or errors when `@-` is off main), all as a final pass *outside* the `op_restore` revert region; replaces `ensure_at_on_main`; new `--rebase` flag; README `### sync` docs + examples [[113]]
147	- feat: single-mode sync + revert command (0.67.0) — plain `vc-x1 sync` is one atomic operation (fetch, converge bookmark, reposition `@`; `--no-check` gone, `--check` a hidden deprecated alias for push preflight); failures stop for inspection with each repo's pre-sync op id persisted to `.vc-x1/sync-state.toml`; new `vc-x1 revert` restores from the snapshots; TDD via the two-clone `tests/cli_sync.rs` regression test of the t1A/t1B scenario [[114]]
148	- docs: todo cleanup + trapezoid entries (0.67.1) — push-related todos reshaped around the trapezoidal (merge non-ff) workflow: new #1 bookmark-invariant fix and #2 push pause point; "record uncovered code commits (N:1)" re-scoped to code worked outside vc-x1; `push --squash` demoted to todo-backlog.md; cycle-protocol.md push-wrapper list synced [[115]]
149

python3 - <<'EOF'
# --- move three Done bullets TODO.md -> done.md, renumbering refs ---
todo = open('TODO.md').read()
done = open('notes/done.md').read()

bullets = []
for tag, old, new in [("0.68.0", "[[23]]", "[[116]]"),
                      ("0.68.1", "[[25]]", "[[117]]"),
                      ("0.69.0", "[[20]]", "[[118]]")]:
    start = todo.index("- feat: pin bot repo to main" if tag == "0.68.0"
                       else "- docs: diagnose silent session-push loss" if tag == "0.68.1"
                       else "- feat: inline session push + squash-push")
    end = todo.index("\n", start)
    line = todo[start:end]
    bullets.append(line.replace(old, new))
    todo = todo[:start] + todo[end+1:]

done = done.replace("""(0.67.1) [[115]]

# References""",
"""(0.67.1) [[115]]
""" + "\n".join(bullets) + """

# References""")
done = done.replace("""[115]: /notes/chores/chores-13.md#docs-todo-cleanup--trapezoid-entries-0671""",

## feat: bot-session transcript viewer

input.new_string:
## feat: bot-session transcript viewer

Commits: [[37]],[[38]],[[39]],[[40]]

Display one Claude Code session transcript
(`.claude/<uuid>.jsonl`) as a readable conversation — first step
toward seeing all bot sessions and linking prompts to the
changes they produced. v1 is file-path in, conversation view
out; index view, session discovery, and cross-file references
(sidechain `agent-*.jsonl`, compaction chains) come in later
cycles.

- Own tolerant reader over any external crate: the transcript
  format is undocumented and churns continuously, so every
  full-schema crate permanently trails (surveyed: cct parses
  today's files but silently drops newer fields, stale since
  2026-05; weavr models the right tolerant posture). The
  long-term goal — prompt ↔ commit linking via ochids — is
  vc-x1-specific anyway.
- Two-layer parse: serde_json as JSON-text → `Value` only (no
  derive anywhere); hand-written extraction into our own
  structs. Unknown fields ride along in the retained raw
  `Value`; unknown entry/block types land in `Other` variants;
  a live session's truncated last line is a warning, never a
  failure. `FileTranscript` is one file's parse — a *session*
  can span files; assembly is a later layer. Whole-file
  in-memory by design (largest observed ~8 MB); streaming via
  `BufRead` is a drop-in escape hatch.
- Renamed `show-session` → `bot-session` mid-cycle: unique
  `b<tab>` completion prefix, display-family naming (list /
  desc / chid / show name what they display), and "bot"
  matches the project's bot-repo terminology. The pushed
  -0/-1 titles keep the old stem.
- Output is eight composable items — headers, user, assistant,
  tool, thinking, results, meta, summary — each `--<item>` /
  `--no-<item>` (last-wins), `--all` / `--none` bulk bases
  (aliases `--no-none` / `--no-all`). Defaults resolve
  git-style, most specific wins: CLI > workspace
  `.vc-config.toml` > user `~/.config/vc-x1/config.toml` >
  built-in (`headers,user,assistant,tool,summary`), both
  configs via `[bot-session].items` (comma-separated string —
  toml_simple has no arrays).
- `--lines SPEC` slices the rendered output: `N` first / `-N`
  last / `I,C` from 0-based Index `I` / `I,-C` ending at `I`;
  `0` = summary only; elision markers at cut points; a sliced
  summary leads with "K of M lines shown" so it never claims
  more than was displayed.
- Plain-text output — the `===` header delimiters carry the
  structure, no ANSI. Headers carry the full UTC date-time;
  the `Z` is claimed only when the source timestamp ends in
  `Z` (observed always across all ~56k lines, but
  undocumented — we think Claude Code writes
  `Date.toISOString()`); any other shape passes through
  verbatim.
- v1 uses no version-control code; when later cycles link
  prompts to commits (chids, ochid trailers), they go through
  jj-lib in-process per the typed jj facade Todo #1 — no new
  `run("jj", …)` sites.
- Deferred / recorded along the way: `--raw` mode (Todo #12,
  source-line units); the As-built-ladder `[[N]]` ref
  convention (Todo #11, shared-doc sync); the repo-wide
  `info!`/`println!` EPIPE panic (Bugs #4), surfaced by the
  first long-output subcommand.

### As-built ladder

- [[37]] 0.70.0-0 chore: open show-session cycle
- [[38]] 0.70.0-1 feat: transcript parse + typed layer for show-session
- [[39]] 0.70.0-2 feat: bot-session command + conversation renderer
- [[40]] 0.70.0-3 feat: bot-session item flags + config defaults
- 0.70.0 feat: bot-session transcript viewer (close-out; ref
  backfills next push)

`0.70.0` close-out work complete. Please review.

What the close-out commit contains:

**Documentation (the emphasis):**
- `README.md` — new `### bot-session` section, placed first among the command sections (display family): the eight-item table with defaults, toggle semantics (`--<item>`/`--no-<item>` last-wins, `--all`/`--none` + aliases), the four-layer git-style config resolution with a TOML example covering both config homes, a worked example block (default view, `--all`, prompts-only, subtraction, all five `--lines` forms), the flag table, and the elision-marker/sliced-summary/UTC-`Z` behavior notes. Plus a `bot-session` line in the usage list.
- `notes/README.md` — inspected, no change needed: it documents the notes directory, not commands.

**Cycle bookkeeping:**
- `notes/chores/chores-13.md` — the cycle section filled: `Commits: [[37]],[[38]],[[39]],[[40]]` (all four rungs are already permanent on `main`, so they backfill now), the problem statement + conceptual design bullets (crate survey → own tolerant reader, two-layer parse, the rename rationale, item model + config hierarchy, `--lines` grammar, UTC-`Z` honesty, jj-lib constraint, deferred items), and an `### As-built ladder` with per-rung `[[N]]` refs per the new convention — the close-out rung listed ref-less until the next push backfills it.
- `TODO.md` — In Progress block moved out (explainer remains); Done entry `feat: bot-session transcript viewer [[3]]` added; the three older push-related Done entries (0.68.0, 0.68.1, 0.69.0) migrated to `notes/done.md` as `[[116]]`-`[[118]]` — their original numbers 20/23/25 were already taken in done.md's namespace, so they took the next free slots (the AGENTS "preserve the number" default hit its first collision); refs 20/23/25 pruned from TODO.md.
- `Cargo.toml`/`Cargo.lock` — `0.70.0`.

**Validation (mandatory full cycle):** fmt applied, clippy `-D warnings` clean, all 6 test suites green, installed — `vc-x1 -V` reports `0.70.0`; `validate-todo` (12 entries sequential) and `validate-bot` (main published) clean; installed binary sanity-run on a real transcript.

Proposed close-out title: `feat: bot-session transcript viewer`. After your review and the description review, the push-shape question (squash / merge non-ff / keep separate) is yours to call at push time.

message.content: Why cap results at 10?

content.text:
Honestly: it's a judgment call from the original renderer design, not a measured number. The reasoning was that tool results are the bulkiest content in a transcript (build logs, file dumps — in the sample file, 93 results across 99 turns), and in a *conversation* view a result serves as context for what the assistant said next, not as a record to re-read — so show enough to recognize what came back (first ~10 lines) plus an honest `… (+N lines)` marker. Ten was picked as "enough to recognize, not enough to drown the dialogue"; it could as defensibly be 5 or 20.

The cap does expose a real gap, though: there is currently **no way to see a full tool result** — even `--all` caps at 10. If you want the complete record today, nothing in the item model reaches it (that's `--raw` territory, or `jq` on the source line). Options if you want this addressed:

1. **Leave it** — the conversation view stays a digest; full fidelity arrives with `--raw` (Todo #12). Zero work now.
2. **`--result-lines N`** (0 = unlimited) — a small knob in the "Output range" family; default stays 10. Modest work, fits the control-everything philosophy.
3. **Uncap under `--all`** — "everything" then really means everything. Cheap, but makes `--all` output enormous and conflates "which items" with "how much of each".

My lean: option 1 for this cycle (close-out is bookkeeping-only by protocol), with option 2 noted as a bullet on Todo #12 so the "how much" knob gets designed alongside `--raw`'s unit questions. Want me to add that bullet?

message.content: For this as-is is fine, but add --result-lines N (0 - unlimited) to #12 and make #12 #1 :)

s = open('TODO.md').read()
entry = """12. **bot-session --raw mode.** Verbatim source lines
    (`cat`-like, no rendering) but honoring `--lines` —
    whose unit then becomes *source JSONL lines*, matching
    the malformed-line warnings and what jq/editors see, not
    rendered lines. Needs its own small design pass (unit
    switch must be explicit in help; summary suppressed or
    to stderr in raw mode). Could ride with the index-view
    cycle.
"""
assert entry in s
s = s.replace(entry, "")
new_entry = """1. **bot-session --raw mode.** Verbatim source lines
    (`cat`-like, no rendering) but honoring `--lines` —
    whose unit then becomes *source JSONL lines*, matching
    the malformed-line warnings and what jq/editors see, not
    rendered lines. Needs its own small design pass (unit
    switch must be explicit in help; summary suppressed or
    to stderr in raw mode). Could ride with the index-view
    cycle.
    - Also add `--result-lines N` (0 = unlimited) — the
      "how much of each item" knob for [result] bodies
      (today hardwired to 10, even under --all); design it
      alongside --raw's unit questions.
"""
old_first = "1. **Refactor: typed jj facade"
assert old_first in s
s = s.replace(old_first, new_entry + old_first)
open('TODO.md', 'w').write(s)
EOF
vc-x1 fix-todo --no-dry-run && vc-x1 validate-todo

1	# Todo
2	
3	This file contains near term tasks with a short description
4	and reference links to more details.
5	
6	Intro paragraphs in `## Todo` and `## Bugs` should begin every
7	line with 1 leading space so they don't match the `^\d+\. `
8	pattern that locates numbered entries; 2 or 3 spaces also work.
9	
10	## In Progress
11	
12	When a `## Todo` item is picked up, its text moves here: the
13	problem overview and its list of things to do. That is followed
14	by the "plan" — a bulleted list of the development "ladder":
15	   - 0.xx.y-0 blah (done)
16	   - 0.xx.y-1 blah blah (current)
17	   - 0.xx.y-2 blah blah blah
18	   - 0.xx.y close-out and validation
19	
20	## Todo
21	
22	 Entries are in **strict priority rank** — #1 highest,
23	 descending. Reprioritize by moving an entry, then
24	 `vc-x1 fix-todo --no-dry-run TODO.md` to renumber.
25	 The numbers are positional rank, not stable IDs — to refer
26	 to a Todo, name it by its **title** (a greppable mention;
27	 a numbered list item has no anchor to link to), not its
28	 number. Long-tail entries
29	 live in [todo-backlog.md](notes/todo-backlog.md). Use the
30	 [Prose Form in AGENTS.md](/AGENTS.md#prose-form); deeper
31	 detail goes in `notes/chores/chores-NN.md` design
32	 subsections (link via `[N]` ref).
33	
34	1. **bot-session --raw mode.** Verbatim source lines
35	   (`cat`-like, no rendering) but honoring `--lines` —
36	   whose unit then becomes *source JSONL lines*, matching
37	   the malformed-line warnings and what jq/editors see, not
38	   rendered lines. Needs its own small design pass (unit
39	   switch must be explicit in help; summary suppressed or
40	   to stderr in raw mode). Could ride with the index-view
41	   cycle.

43	     "how much of each item" knob for [result] bodies
44	     (today hardwired to 10, even under --all); design it
45	     alongside --raw's unit questions.
46	2. **Refactor: typed jj facade → jj-lib in-process; end
47	   subprocess spawning.** Version-control operations are
48	   ~30 hand-rolled `run("jj", …)` spawns plus every
49	   mutation, with per-module private wrappers and raw-git
50	   vestiges in init — stderr parsing instead of typed
51	   errors, and jj's single-attempt index-lock acquisition
52	   (the push `bookmark-set` lock race in [bugs.md](notes/bugs.md))
53	   can't be retried where it fails. A multi-ladder program;
54	   the staged plan, design detail, and the eight absorbed
55	   former Todos live in
56	   [refactor-20260716.md](notes/refactor-20260716.md).
57	   - Stages in execution order: DRY facade → hygiene
58	     riders → facade owns topology → de-gitify init →
59	     split push.rs → jj-lib migration → push body-intro
60	     validation → trapezoid close-out → por → dual
61	     conversion.
62	3. **vc-x1 push: pause point between commit and publish
63	   stages.** The merge non-ff close-out is a three-step
64	   sequence:
65	   - commit the close-out pair locally (normal 1:1 commit
66	     stages, ochids injected both directions)
67	   - rebase the code-side close-out into the merge (chids
68	     survive rebase, so every ochid stays valid)
69	   - publish
70	
71	   Push has no supported stop after `commit-bot`, so today
72	   the recipe pre-commits both sides manually and resumes via
73	   `--from bookmark-set --yes` — skipping exactly the stages
74	   that inject `ochid:` trailers.

76	     or `--no-publish`; name open); the existing `--from

88	     is the end state; this pause point remains the manual
89	     path until it lands.
90	
91	4. **Version-number protocol is fragile — versions are
92	   baked into titles/bodies/todo/done/chores before the
93	   change lands.** The cycle protocol embeds an `X.Y.Z-N`
94	   version in commit titles and bodies, `## Todo` /
95	   `## Done` entries, and chores headers — all written
96	   while the work is in progress, i.e. before it lands.
97	   But version numbers are subject to change: in a public,
98	   merge-based flow (e.g. Linux), the version a change
99	   ships under is only fixed when it merges into `main`,
100	   so the landing version can't be anticipated while the
101	   work is underway. Pervasive version-in-text is

input.command:
python3 - <<'EOF'
s = open('TODO.md').read()
start = s.index("1. **bot-session --raw mode.**")
end = s.index("2. **vc-x1 push: pause point")
s = s[:start] + s[end:]

block = """**feat: bot-session --raw mode + --result-lines**

Two "how much" knobs the item model deliberately left out.
`--raw`: verbatim source lines (`cat`-like, no rendering) but
honoring `--lines` — whose unit then becomes *source JSONL
lines*, matching the malformed-line warnings and what
jq/editors see, not rendered lines; the unit switch must be
explicit in help, and raw output is verbatim-only (no summary,
no elision markers). `--result-lines N` (0 = unlimited): the
[result]-body cap, today hardwired to 10 even under --all.

- 0.70.1-0 chore: open bot-session --raw cycle (current)
- 0.70.1-1 feat: bot-session --result-lines knob
- 0.70.1-2 feat: bot-session --raw mode
- 0.70.1 close-out and validation

"""
anchor = s.index("## Todo")
s = s[:anchor] + block + s[anchor:]
open('TODO.md', 'w').write(s)
EOF
vc-x1 fix-todo --no-dry-run 2>&1 | tail -3 && vc-x1 validate-todo | tail -1

48	 subsections (link via `[N]` ref).
49	
50	1. **vc-x1 push: pause point between commit and publish
51	   stages.** The merge non-ff close-out is a three-step
52	   sequence:
53	   - commit the close-out pair locally (normal 1:1 commit
54	     stages, ochids injected both directions)
55	   - rebase the code-side close-out into the merge (chids
56	     survive rebase, so every ochid stays valid)
57	   - publish
58	
59	   Push has no supported stop after `commit-bot`, so today
60	   the recipe pre-commits both sides manually and resumes via
61	   `--from bookmark-set --yes` — skipping exactly the stages
62	   that inject `ochid:` trailers.
63	   - Add a stop after the commit stages (`--to commit-bot`
64	     or `--no-publish`; name open); the existing `--from
65	     bookmark-set` is already the resume half.
66	   - Retires the close-out workaround; the merge commit
67	     carries its code→bot ochid because it was injected
68	     normally, before the rebase.
69	   - Together with the Todo "push/sync: bookmark is
70	     code-repo-only; pin the bot repo to main", completes
71	     the trapezoidal-commit workflow (1:1 bot↔code
72	     throughout; the merge is a code-side-only shape
73	     operation).
74	   - Interim recipe only: the refactor program's
75	     [trapezoid close-out stage](notes/refactor-20260716.md#stage-trapezoid-close-out)
76	     is the end state; this pause point remains the manual
77	     path until it lands.
78	
79	2. **Refactor: typed jj facade → jj-lib in-process; end
80	   subprocess spawning.** Version-control operations are
81	   ~30 hand-rolled `run("jj", …)` spawns plus every
82	   mutation, with per-module private wrappers and raw-git
83	   vestiges in init — stderr parsing instead of typed
84	   errors, and jj's single-attempt index-lock acquisition
85	   (the push `bookmark-set` lock race in [bugs.md](notes/bugs.md))
86	   can't be retried where it fails. A multi-ladder program;
87	   the staged plan, design detail, and the eight absorbed
88	   former Todos live in
89	   [refactor-20260716.md](notes/refactor-20260716.md).
90	   - Stages in execution order: DRY facade → hygiene
91	     riders → facade owns topology → de-gitify init →
92	     split push.rs → jj-lib migration → push body-intro
93	     validation → trapezoid close-out → por → dual
94	     conversion.
95	3. **Version-number protocol is fragile — versions are
96	   baked into titles/bodies/todo/done/chores before the
97	   change lands.** The cycle protocol embeds an `X.Y.Z-N`
98	   version in commit titles and bodies, `## Todo` /
99	   `## Done` entries, and chores headers — all written
100	   while the work is in progress, i.e. before it lands.
101	   But version numbers are subject to change: in a public,
102	   merge-based flow (e.g. Linux), the version a change
103	   ships under is only fixed when it merges into `main`,
104	   so the landing version can't be anticipated while the
105	   work is underway. Pervasive version-in-text is
106	   therefore fragile for any non-linear / multi-contributor
107	   workflow. Promoted from Ideas at 0.65.2-0; slated for
108	   the cycle after 0.65.2.
109	   - Open question: what identifies a cycle's commits if
110	     not a pre-assigned version?
111	     - Needs to be unique within some agreed upon domain.
112	       A contributors email address would do it, but also
113	       a UUID (short-version) for a contribution. I could
114	       imagine a UUID generated from the initial email/issue
115	       that and then "version number" schema appended to that.
116	   - Surfaces to update once the identifier is chosen:
117	     cycle-protocol.md (title shape, Numbering), AGENTS.md
118	     (commit-recording headers), and the `vc-x1` validators
119	     that parse `(X.Y.Z)` strings.
120	4. **sync follow-up: extract `move-bookmark` command.** The
121	   "put the bookmark / `@` where it belongs" step at the end
122	   of sync (reposition logic) is useful standalone — e.g. the
123	   t1B scenario where `main` is right but `@` isn't on it —
124	   and deserves an honestly-named command instead of a mode.
125	   - `vc-x1 move-bookmark` (name open): no fetch; move `@`
126	     (and optionally the bookmark) onto a target under the
127	     same safety rules as sync's reposition step.
128	   - Sync's final step becomes a call to the same logic.
129	   - Follow-up to the 0.67.0 single-mode sync cycle.
130	5. **sync follow-up: push preflight in-process; drop
131	   `--check`; revisit push auto-rollback.** Push's preflight
132	   shells out to `vc-x1 sync --check` — a verify-only pass
133	   that is both racy (remote can move before the user's

python3 - <<'EOF'
s = open('TODO.md').read()
block = """**feat: bot-session --raw mode + --result-lines**

Two "how much" knobs the item model deliberately left out.
`--raw`: verbatim source lines (`cat`-like, no rendering) but
honoring `--lines` — whose unit then becomes *source JSONL
lines*, matching the malformed-line warnings and what
jq/editors see, not rendered lines; the unit switch must be
explicit in help, and raw output is verbatim-only (no summary,
no elision markers). `--result-lines N` (0 = unlimited): the
[result]-body cap, today hardwired to 10 even under --all.

Two lightweight single-commit cycles (no Preparation):

- 0.70.1 feat: bot-session --result-lines knob (current)
- 0.70.2 feat: bot-session --raw mode

"""
# undo the mid-sentence splice
bad = "Intro paragraphs in `" + block + "## Todo` and `## Bugs` should begin every"
assert bad in s, "splice not found"
s = s.replace(bad, "Intro paragraphs in `## Todo` and `## Bugs` should begin every")
# insert correctly: after the In Progress explainer, before the ## Todo section header
anchor = "   - 0.xx.y close-out and validation\n\n## Todo\n"
assert anchor in s
s = s.replace(anchor, "   - 0.xx.y close-out and validation\n\n" + block + "## Todo\n")
open('TODO.md', 'w').write(s)
EOF
sed -n '1,40p' TODO.md && vc-x1 validate-todo | tail -1

problem overview and its list of things to do. That is followed

   - 0.xx.y-0 blah (done)
   - 0.xx.y-1 blah blah (current)
   - 0.xx.y-2 blah blah blah
   - 0.xx.y close-out and validation

**feat: bot-session --raw mode + --result-lines**

`--raw`: verbatim source lines (`cat`-like, no rendering) but
honoring `--lines` — whose unit then becomes *source JSONL
lines*, matching the malformed-line warnings and what
jq/editors see, not rendered lines; the unit switch must be
explicit in help, and raw output is verbatim-only (no summary,
no elision markers). `--result-lines N` (0 = unlimited): the
[result]-body cap, today hardwired to 10 even under --all.

Two lightweight single-commit cycles (no Preparation):

- 0.70.2 feat: bot-session --raw mode

## Todo

 Entries are in **strict priority rank** — #1 highest,
 descending. Reprioritize by moving an entry, then
 `vc-x1 fix-todo --no-dry-run TODO.md` to renumber.

1	# Chores-13
2	
3	Continuation of `chores-12.md` (closed at `0.62.x`). This
4	file covers the `0.63.0` cycle onward.
5	
6	Subsection headers that record a release use the
7	trailing-version format from AGENTS.md: `## Description (X.Y.Z)`.
8	Reference numbering is file-local — see
9	[`AGENTS.md`](../../AGENTS.md#reference-numbering); chores-13
10	starts at `[1]`.
11	
12	## docs: adopt AGENTS.md (0.63.0)
13	
14	Commits: [[1]]
15	
16	The bot-instructions file is renamed `CLAUDE.md` → `AGENTS.md`
17	so Zed and the broader agent-tooling ecosystem (which now
18	default to `AGENTS.md`) read it natively. Claude Code still
19	auto-loads only `CLAUDE.md`, so a one-line `CLAUDE.md` import
20	shim (`@AGENTS.md`) keeps it working — both tools read one
21	canonical file.
22	
23	- **Single canonical file** — `AGENTS.md` holds the content;
24	  `CLAUDE.md` is a one-line `@AGENTS.md` import for Claude
25	  Code's loader. Nothing human-facing points at the shim.
26	- **Live references repointed** — every `CLAUDE.md` mention
27	  and `#anchor` link in live docs (`README.md`,
28	  `ARCHITECTURE.md`, `notes/`, the `src/push.rs` pre-commit
29	  doc comment, and `AGENTS.md` itself) now names `AGENTS.md`,
30	  so links resolve in editors and on GitHub.
31	- **History left as written** — `CLAUDE.md` prose in
32	  `chores-01..12` and `done.md` records what was true at the
33	  time and is untouched; only the live navigational anchor
34	  links in the `chores-10/11/12` headers
35	  (`#reference-numbering`) were repointed so they don't
36	  dangle against the one-line `CLAUDE.md` shim.
37	
38	### Why an import shim, not a symlink
39	
40	A symlink `CLAUDE.md → AGENTS.md` also satisfies Claude
41	Code's loader, but the `@AGENTS.md` import was chosen
42	because it is explicit and visible in a diff and does not
43	depend on symlink semantics (git symlink handling,
44	cross-platform checkout). Separately, GitHub renders a
45	symlinked `.md` as a stub page rather than the target's
46	markdown, so a `CLAUDE.md#anchor` link through a symlink
47	would not resolve — moot here since live links now point
48	straight at `AGENTS.md`, but it reinforces keeping
49	`CLAUDE.md` a real file.
50	
51	## docs: tighten after-finalize rule (0.63.1)
52	
53	Commits: [[2]]
54	
55	The stop-and-wait rule in `cycle-protocol.md` was titled
56	`### After finalize: stop and wait` and phrased as "final
57	words go in the approval prompt *before* executing finalize"
58	— wording that assumes finalize is a separate,
59	manually-invoked step and names only finalize as the
60	trigger. With `vc-x1 push` the push and `vc-x1 finalize` on
61	`.claude` are bundled as the tail stages, so there is no
62	"before finalize" gap left to speak in once the push is
63	invoked. After `0.63.0`'s push the bot emitted a closing
64	summary *after* `vc-x1 push` returned, violating the rule
65	(and likely riding into the `.claude` commit via the
66	`--delay 10` window).
67	
68	- **Renamed to "After push or finalize"** — both triggers
69	  are named, in the title and the paragraph: the remote
70	  crossing is the push itself, and `vc-x1 finalize` on
71	  `.claude` is the detached step. This also covers a manual
72	  `jj git push` (push, no finalize) and a manual
73	  `vc-x1 finalize` (no wrapper).
74	- **Wrapper case spelled out** — `vc-x1 push` does both as
75	  tail stages (`push-app`, then `vc-x1 finalize` on
76	  `.claude`): closing words go *before* invoking the wrapper,
77	  and the bot does not purposely emit anything after it
78	  returns. The rule names the `vc-x1 finalize` operation, not
79	  the wrapper's internal `finalize-claude` stage label.
80	
81	The bot thinks the original lapse was a compliance slip (the
82	rule was read and quoted, then broken), not a comprehension
83	gap; the doc change closes the one genuine ambiguity (the
84	wrapper bundles push + finalize, leaving no gap to speak in)
85	rather than expecting prose alone to prevent the slip. The
86	rule lives only in `cycle-protocol.md`; AGENTS.md already
87	mandates reading it before commit work, so no duplicate
88	restatement was added there.
89	
90	## docs: codify merge-non-ff recipe (0.64.0)
91	
92	Commits: [[3]]
93	
94	Multi-commit cycles default to the merge-non-ff trapezoid,
95	but the recipe is split between `cycle-protocol.md` and
96	AGENTS.md with wrinkles re-derived each time (exercised
97	manually on `0.62.0`). Codify the full recipe and
98	standardize the flag spelling. Split out of Idea #1 (the
99	broader cycle-protocol.md codification).
100	
101	- Recipe: `jj rebase -r <closeout> --onto <prev-closeout>
102	  --onto <work-tip>` (first `--onto` = trunk → first
103	  parent), then `jj new <merge>` to lift `@` above the
104	  merge, then `jj git push --bookmark main`.
105	- Standardize on `--onto` / `-o` (canonical), not the
106	  `-d` alias — update AGENTS.md's `-d` spellings (jj
107	  Basics + the post-amend `jj new` note) to `--onto`.
108	- `@` reverts to the work-tip's content until the
109	  `jj new` (the "post-amend `jj new`" gotcha already in
110	  AGENTS.md).
111	- Post-hoc caveat: if the cycle was already pushed
112	  keep-separate (as `0.62.0` was), the rebase needs
113	  `--ignore-immutable` and the push is a force-update of
114	  `main`; the standard recipe assumes the merge is set up
115	  before the close-out push.
116	
117	### As-built ladder
118	
119	- 0.64.0-0 Preparation — version bump, 0.63.1 `Commits:`
120	  backfill, item pickup. Also clarified the Preparation
121	  step itself: a Cargo.lock-update bullet, reworded the
122	  Todo "move into In Progress", and a Prose-form intro
123	  line at the top of `cycle-protocol.md`.
124	- 0.64.0-1 cycle-protocol.md — promote the recipe to a
125	  `### Merge non-ff recipe` subsection (rebase → `jj new`
126	  lift → push, with the parents broken out as sub-bullets
127	  and a post-hoc caveat); trim the shape bullet to point
128	  at it. Also reword `### Shape at close-out push`: the
129	  intro reframed (work done, shape chosen at push,
130	  post-publish change is a remote rewrite) and Merge
131	  non-ff tagged as the current default.
132	- 0.64.0-2 AGENTS.md — standardize `-d` → `--onto` in the
133	  post-amend note; add a jj Basics bullet for the
134	  `--onto`/`-o` spelling; sub-bullet the post-amend
135	  command list; reframe the note around jj's empty-`@`
136	  final form, dropping the merge-specific example and the
137	  HEAD-detach mechanism (the recipe now owns the merge
138	  sequence).
139	- 0.64.0-3 review round — make `### Merge non-ff recipe`
140	  step 2 self-contained (own the empty-`@` why inline) and
141	  drop the AGENTS.md post-amend `jj new` note entirely. The
142	  note's non-merge cases proved mis-categorized: `jj squash

145	  to restore). One-directional ref (Shape → recipe), no

1	# vc-x1
2	
3	- [Overview](#vc-x1)
4	- [Architecture](ARCHITECTURE.md)
5	- [Terminology](#terminology)
6	- [Usage](#usage)
7	  - [Revision shortcuts](#revision-shortcuts)
8	  - [Shell completion](#shell-completion)
9	  - [validate-desc](#validate-desc)
10	  - [fix-desc](#fix-desc)
11	  - [validate-todo](#validate-todo)
12	  - [fix-todo](#fix-todo)
13	  - [validate-bot](#validate-bot)
14	  - [clone](#clone)
15	  - [init](#init)
16	  - [symlink](#symlink)
17	  - [sync](#sync)
18	  - [revert](#revert)
19	  - [squash-push](#squash-push)
20	  - [push](#push)
21	  - [Testing push + squash-push](#testing-push--squash-push)
22	  - [Testing the ochid-trailer guard](#testing-the-ochid-trailer-guard)
23	- [Cross-repo Linking with Git Trailers](#cross-repo-linking-with-git-trailers)
24	- [jj Tips for Git Users](#jj-tips-for-git-users)
25	- [Thoughts for the future](#thoughts-for-the-future)
26	- [Contributing](#contributing)
27	- [License](#license)
28	
29	This is experiment 1 to explore creating a Vibe Coding (vc) environment.
30	We will investigate ways of using the dual jj-git repo concept, explored
31	in [hw-jjg-bot](https://github.com/winksaville/hw-jjg-bot.git) to
32	initially make it easy to see how the code base evolved. This is
33	made possible by the fact that we have two repos one with the code
34	and one with the conversation with the bot.
35	
36	I've chosen the jj-git environment because jj provides the concept that
37	each commit has an immutable changeID as well as the mutable commitID
38	of git. The idea is that each commit made on repo A writes the
39	changeID in the commit message to repo B. Thus there is a cross reference
40	between the two repos and this will allow vc-x1 to show how the repo
41	evolved and the entity (bot or human) can more clearly understand **how** and
42	most importantly **why** the code evolved.
43	
44	The solution space is wide open, from trivial CLI, web or app based
45	(mobile/non-mobile). In addition, I could see this as an extension to
46	existing programming editors like vscode and zed or even creating our
47	own IDE for vc.
48	
49	See [Initial commit with dual jj-git repos](./notes/chores/chores-01.md#initial-commit-with-dual-jj-git-repos)
50	for how the initial commit was created with the dual jj-git repos. After
51	doing so and I then created this README.md file.
52	
53	## Terminology
54	
55	A vc-x1 workspace pairs two jj-git repos, and this README names
56	their two sides **work** and **bot**:
57	
58	- **work repo** — the repo at the project root, holding the work
59	  product itself (source code, docs — the repo you would have
60	  anyway).
61	- **bot repo** — the `.claude` repo nested inside it, holding the
62	  bot's session data (the conversation that produced the work).
63	- **work** / **bot** — the two sides as roles, used wherever a
64	  command or doc needs to say which repo it means (e.g. push's
65	  `commit-work` / `commit-bot` stages).
66	
67	One historical wrinkle: the `-s`/`--scope` flag still spells the
68	work side `code` (`-s code`, `-s code,bot`) — that keyword predates
69	the work/bot naming.
70	
71	## Usage
72	
73	```
74	vc-x1 list [-r REVISION] [-n COMMITS]  # List commits in a jj repo
75	vc-x1 desc [-r REVISION] [-n COMMITS]  # Show full description of a commit
76	vc-x1 chid [-r REVISION] [-n COMMITS]  # Print changeID(s) for a revision
77	vc-x1 show [-r REVISION] [-n COMMITS]  # Show commit details and diff summary
78	vc-x1 bot-session <FILE> [OPTS]        # Display a session transcript as a conversation
79	vc-x1 validate-desc [OPTS]                 # Validate commit descriptions
80	vc-x1 fix-desc [OPTS]                     # Fix commit descriptions (dry-run default)
81	vc-x1 validate-todo [FILE]                # Check todo-file entry numbering
82	vc-x1 fix-todo [FILE]                     # Renumber todo file (dry-run default)
83	vc-x1 validate-bot [OPTS]                 # Check the bot repo is published
84	vc-x1 clone <REPO> [NAME] [OPTS]          # Clone a dual-repo project
85	vc-x1 init <TARGET> [OPTS]                # Create a new dual-repo project
86	vc-x1 symlink [TARGET] [OPTS]             # Create Claude Code project symlink
87	vc-x1 sync [OPTS]                          # Fetch + sync both repos to their remotes
88	vc-x1 revert [OPTS]                        # Restore repos to a prior operation
89	vc-x1 squash-push [BOOKMARK] [OPTS]        # Squash @ into @-, advance a bookmark, push
90	vc-x1 push [BOOKMARK] [OPTS]               # Commit both repos, push work, squash-push bot
91	vc-x1 --version                            # Print version
92	vc-x1 --help                           # Print help
93	```
94	
95	**The `..` notation:** dots on the revision show which direction to
96	list commits:
97	
98	- `x..` — x at top, ancestors below (older commits)
99	- `..x` — descendants above (newer commits), x at bottom
100	- `..x..` — both directions, x in the middle
101	
102	COMMITS is the total number of commits to show (x is always included).
103	Without dots, `x` with a count defaults to `x..` (ancestors).
104	For `..x..`, the budget is split: ancestors get the extra on odd counts.
105	
106	```
107	vc-x1 list -r @              # just @ (1 commit)
108	vc-x1 list -r @.. -n 5       # @ + 4 ancestors (5 commits, @ at top)
109	vc-x1 list -r ..@ -n 3       # 2 descendants + @ (3 commits, @ at bottom)
110	vc-x1 list -r ..@.. -n 3     # 1 descendant, @, 1 ancestor (3 commits)
111	vc-x1 list -r ..@.. -n 4     # 1 descendant, @, 2 ancestors (4 commits)
112	```
113	
114	### Shell completion
115	
116	vc-x1 provides tab completion for all subcommands and flags using claps
117	[unstable-dynamic](https://docs.rs/clap_complete/latest/clap_complete/env/struct.CompleteEnv.html)
118	only feature. It is a "simple" implementation and does not handle completing
119	revisions but still useful. To enable it, add one of the following to your shell's startup file:
120	
121	```bash
122	# bash (~/.bashrc)
123	source <(COMPLETE=bash vc-x1)
124	
125	# zsh (~/.zshrc)
126	source <(COMPLETE=zsh vc-x1)
127	
128	# fish (~/.config/fish/config.fish)
129	source (COMPLETE=fish vc-x1 | psub)
130	```
131	
132	Completions are generated dynamically by the binary, so they stay in
133	sync with the installed version automatically.
134	
135	### Positional shorthand
136	
137	REVISION and COMMITS can be given as positional arguments, omitting
138	`-r` and `-n`:
139	
140	```
141	vc-x1 list x                # just x (1 commit)
142	vc-x1 list x 5              # x.. 5 → x + 4 ancestors (5 commits)
143	vc-x1 list x.. 5            # same as above
144	vc-x1 list ..x 3            # 2 descendants + x (3 commits)
145	vc-x1 list ..x.. 3          # 1 descendant, x, 1 ancestor (3 commits)
146	vc-x1 list ..x.. 1          # just x (1 commit)
147	```
148	
149	**Defaults:** with no arguments, REVISION is `@` and COMMITS is 1
150	(just the working copy).
151	
152	Named flags `-r`/`--revision`, `-n`/`--commits`, `-R`/`--repo`, and
153	`-s`/`--scope` take precedence over positional arguments.
154	
155	### Multi-repo queries
156	
157	`-s`/`--scope` selects workspace sides by role keyword; `code` is the
158	work repo, `bot` is the `.claude` repo, `code,bot` is both. The
159	workspace root is found by walking up from cwd (the existing
160	`find_workspace_root` rule).
161	
162	```
163	vc-x1 chid -s code,bot          # both sides of the workspace
164	vc-x1 chid -s code              # just the work repo
165	vc-x1 chid -s bot               # just the bot repo
166	vc-x1 list @.. 3 -s code,bot    # works with chid / desc / list / show
167	```
168	
169	`-R`/`--repo` takes a single path. Used alone it points at one jj
170	repo (workspace lookup is skipped); combined with `-s` it overrides
171	the workspace root for the role lookup:
172	
173	```
174	vc-x1 chid -R .                  # single repo at .
175	vc-x1 chid -R .claude            # single repo at .claude
176	vc-x1 chid -R ../other -s code,bot   # both sides of ../other workspace
177	```
178	
179	Defaults preserve prior behavior — no flag → `[.]`, `-R foo` alone
180	→ `[foo]`. When multiple repos are resolved (any `-s` that names
181	more than one role), output is labeled with bold `=== path ===`

message.content: I'm thinking ./vc-config.toml should have all settable fields with there defaults so it always upto date?

content.text:
Interesting idea — let me separate what it gets right from a trap hiding in it:

**The goal is discoverability**: open `.vc-config.toml` and see everything settable. Worth having.

**The trap is that explicit values stop being defaults.** If init writes `items = "headers,user,assistant,tool,summary"` as a live key, the workspace has silently *pinned* that choice at generation time. When a later vc-x1 changes the built-in default or adds a ninth item, every old workspace keeps overriding it with the stale value — the file says "default" but acts as an intentional override. And the file can't be "always up to date" by construction: it's a static file, only as current as the binary that wrote it; upgrading vc-x1 doesn't rewrite anyone's committed config.

The classic resolution (sshd_config, postgresql.conf style) is **commented-out defaults**: init emits every settable key with its default value behind a `#`. That documents the full surface in-file, while absent-means-current-default stays true — uncommenting is the explicit act of pinning. Still has the staleness-of-listing problem (an old file won't mention new keys), which is why the stronger companion is:

- **`vc-x1 config --defaults`** (name TBD): print the complete annotated schema *from the installed binary* — derived from code, so it literally cannot drift. Diff it against your file to see what's new; paste what you want to change. Optionally a `validate-config` that flags unknown/misspelled keys, which today are silently ignored.

Right now the settable surface is tiny — `[workspace] path`/`other-repo` plus `[bot-session] items` — so nothing is urgent, but the pattern should be picked before the surface grows. My recommendation: commented defaults in init's generated file + a config-printing subcommand later; never live default values.

The plain version: writing the defaults into the file as real settings would quietly lock them in, so when the tool's defaults improve, old projects wouldn't follow. Writing them as comments shows people what they *can* set without locking anything, and a command that prints the current full list straight from the tool is the only thing that can never go stale.

Want me to add this as a Todo entry (it would ride in the `0.70.1` commit)?

message.content: Yes add the Todo entry, then present the description

    - applies to both config homes (workspace + user)."""
s = s[:anchor] + entry + s[anchor:]
open('TODO.md', 'w').write(s)
